name: Ten Tasty Vegetables (Shi Ziang Cai)
recipeIngredient: 8 Pieces soy spiced beancurd (or Loma Linda brand Vegetable Hamburger)
1/2 cup Nami dried black mushrooms, soaked and shredded
1/2 cup Cloud Ear dried fungus, soaked
1/2 cup Dried lily flowers, soaked and hard tips removed
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare 6 to 8 tablespoons peanut oil for stir-frying.
2. Shred carrots, turnip, Chinese celery, and beancurd into 2-inch lengths.
3. Shred onions, greens, and all other ingredients into 2-inch lengths.
4. Rinse mushrooms, fungus, and lily flowers, then soak them in hot water until they reach the specified amounts. Shred mushrooms and chop fungus.
5. Soak bean thread noodles.
6. Stir-fry fresh vegetables separately with about 1 tablespoon oil for each ingredient in a hot wok. Add salt to taste. Drain off excess water (soybean sprouts should be cooked until slightly charred for fullest flavor). Reserve the liquid.
7. To stir-fry dried soaked ingredients, begin with a hot wok, add 2 to 3 tablespoons oil, then add mushrooms, fungus, and lily flowers. Stir-fry green onions and combine all other ingredients with them, including noodles.
8. Allow the dish to cool before serving.
Note: Fresh or canned bamboo shoot may be substituted for any vegetable. Seaweed may be used instead of some of the fungus.
